ops.title: Gate content validation: shared predicate across layers
ops.body:
# Gate content validation — one predicate, checked at every layer

Empty gate text is invalid training input. A gate sample whose rendered
`gate_text(window)` collapses to bare role markup (`<assistant>\n`) teaches the
gate "empty context ⇒ steer" — the same fire-on-nothing pathology the capture-seam
doc (`c275b631`) fixed for the watcher.

## The single predicate

`cc_steer/rendering.py::has_substantive_gate_content(window)` is the one window-level
truth: `has_substantive_content(watcher_prompt(window))`. It is equivalent **by
construction** to the freeze-eval text check
`GATE_ROLE_BLOCK.sub("", gate_text(window)).strip()` — `test_rendering.py` asserts the
two never disagree. If you add a new gate rendering path, keep them in lockstep.

## Where it is enforced (defense in depth — do not bypass any layer)

1. **Sampler generation.** `negatives.py::event_samples` breaks the rewind loop the
   moment a rewound window renders empty (monotonic: a deeper rewind only loses more).
   `context_rebuild.py::positive_gate_samples` filters the same way, so a
   `rebuild-context` pass never *regenerates* empties either.
2. **Store insert choke point.** `store.py::record_gate_samples` drops any empty sample
   of any kind before insert — the single seam every generator flows through.
3. **Export.** `export.py::gate_row` calls `validated_prompt(..., view="gate")` and
   raises `EmptyWatcherPrompt(view="gate")`, matching `live_gate_row`, so an empty gate
   row aborts the export rather than reaching a parquet.
4. **Freeze.** `retrain/evalset.py::freeze_eval` raises `EmptyEvalContext` (the
   text-regex path).

## The two degenerate populations (why this bites)

- **Rewound-past-content positives.** A steering window shaped
  `before=[assistant(empty), user, user, ...]` (leading zero-length assistant turn is a
  turn-boundary artifact). `gate_text` renders only the model-visible context turns
  (the user steer is the excluded label), so the deepest rewinds drop every
  content-bearing user turn and leave only the empty assistant. Offset 0 is never empty
  for a non-quarantined parent, so **no positive event is lost — only the empty tail
  rewinds are trimmed.**
- **Empty-anchor random negatives.** `sample_windows` occasionally anchors on an
  empty-content assistant turn (`before=[assistant(empty)]`, `trigger=None`); content
  lives only in `after`, which gate rendering correctly ignores.

## Migration

`cc_steer prune-gate-samples` (`context_rebuild.py::prune_empty_gate_samples`) deletes
existing empties: idempotent, DB-only (empties are a pure function of stored
`window_json` — no transcript re-read), run with the watch daemon unloaded, reusing
`store.repair_gate_samples`' single `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` delete path with a
planned-vs-deleted assertion. First production run (2026-07-16) pruned 2627 of 17427
rows (2559 positive_window, 10 hard_negative, 58 random_negative). Gate-sample repair
constraints from the round-4 hardening doc (`8fad267f`) still hold — the prune only
adds a delete-empties pass; it does not touch family reconciliation.
ops.when: When refactoring gate sample generation, export logic, or window truncation
